summary: Superb matrix recording that captures a high‑energy William & Mary show highlighted by Let It Grow, Morning Dew and the opening Half Step.
review: The matrix feed delivers crystal‑clear sound with a vivid “being there” feel, though a splice and occasional flutter in the Not Fade Away/Dawn segment are noted. Musically the band rides an infectious wave of excitement from the half‑step opener through standout performances of Let It Grow, Morning Dew and a scorching Half Step, making this one of the year’s most celebrated shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive fan feedback (nine 4‑5★ reviews) praises both performance and matrix sound quality; only one low rating cites vocal and splice issues, so the combined show/recording score is very high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix with strong ambience; reviewers rave about “mirror finish” and remastering. One detailed review mentions a noticeable AUD splice in the Not Fade Away segment and a brief flutter on one channel that required manual correction.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert bursts out of the gate with an inspired half‑step, sustains momentum through a first‑set highlight (Let It Grow) and builds to a powerful second set where Not Fade Away segues into a scorching Dew. Energy stays high throughout, only a brief vocal dip on Around and Around is noted.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for electrifying guitar tone and boundless energy; one reviewer calls his vocals “awful” due to post‑laryngitis issues.
band_member_comments.Bob: His lead vocal work on El Paso and Let It Grow is highlighted as excellent, plus backing vocals on Bertha are noted positively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Criticized for disappearing in the mix or offering only chordal support; a reviewer misses Keith’s earlier creativity.
